item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks! That was part of the idea, to make it have an actual throughline story instead of just the "we abuse the thing" meanderings of 5109. I like to think I've gotten better at putting coherent narratives where they wouldn't automatically go.

The dissonance was important to me; it was suggested that I lose the HDD one near the end, because it doesn't directly contribute to the "plot," but I felt it was important to show a contrast between what the anomaly was "trying" to do, and what we were trying to do with it. There would have been more on that note, but I didn't want to make this too too long. The format is such that it would get tedious with too many examples; I did cut a few on Grigori's suggestion, which improved it considerably.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You had me at spiritual successor to SCP-5109. I would say this is even better, as it ties the story more together than 5109 did. +1

I do find the dissonance interesting between the fun of "exploiting" the anomaly and the extremes, to which they have to go, to do so.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Once I knew what the idea was, I knew it had to be in the 5109 format. Glad it was recognizably mine :P And glad you liked it! I am a fan of "aha" moments. Especially at the end. I may or may not end a tale at some point in the future with a character just going "Ohhhhhhhhhhh."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i thought i recognized that nested footnote dialogue style. this is another winner from you, harry. i love the "aha" moment they had at the end there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks! That's everything I was going for :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Excellent! The anomaly itself is relatively simple, but leaves itself open for all sorts of fun to be had with how it can be used. The interactions between the characters were entertaining and felt natural, and the little stories contained within them were great!

Easy +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you! I'm glad what I know isn't wrong hahaha

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: God Harry, you always know how to do these stories. +1 very deserved

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks! High praise from an actual artist. I have a lot of fun with the pics, so it's nice to hear that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am obsessed with how you do images, they're always so eye-catching and fun.

item.title: Re: Undeleted! UNDELETED!
item.content:encoded:
My favorite thing about this is that the real story is not the wild little short stories but the commentary after the fact, especially seeing characters I've really grown to love having an evolving argument.

On Guard 43, its some good shit people!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Glad you liked it enough :P It was always going to be something of a follow-up to that format, although I decided to add the pics to keep it from being so wall-of-texty. Whereas 5109 ended with a decisive "okay let's stop this, it's silly," I decided to end this one with "and then they kept doing it." Whether it works or not, or just disrupts the flow, will come down to personal preference.

Thanks for the comment and the vote!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It reads somewhat similarly to your One-Time Password in terms of escalation and applications, and that's definitely not a bad thing - I always enjoy the logical sequence of your works.
If there's a downside to this, it's that the ending does leave me wanting, and it kinda sucks me out of the very enjoyable flow.

Overall, I liked it enough to +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks, notgull :) I'm aware of the overtones on this one particularly, of course; the skip it's a pseudo-sequel to was gonna be a lot more like this originally, but it was only my second post so I was too afraid. I think I've reached the point where I can call my researchers being dickbags a stylistic choice lol

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It’s always a joy to read your material, Harry. It’s a bit thick on lol!Foundation, but I enjoyed it nonetheless.

+1

item.title: Undeleted! UNDELETED!
item.content:encoded:
One of the longest gestation periods I've had for a skip. This one was in my head before ETTRA was a thing, before Dr. Dan lived again. Luckily, I think those things provided a focus the idea needed.

This is pretty obviously a spiritual successor, or maybe even a direct sequel, to SCP-5109: "The One-Time Password." It still stands alone, though.

Thanks to Grigori Karpin and Placeholder McD for their crit!
